title: Increase viewable space in Academy training
excerpt: I’m following your training in your academy but I’m really annoyed by the fact that whatever I do, the useable space of the website stays tiny. In Chrome and Firefox, the lower part of the website is persistently filling…

posts.author: Just1n (🍉 Justin 🍉)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’m following your training in your academy but I’m really annoyed by the fact that whatever I do, the useable space of the website stays tiny. In Chrome and Firefox, the lower part of the website is persistently filling much or most of the screen (depending on how far you’ve zoomed in/out). I want more space to read the exercises and not have to scroll so much. Right now there is vertical space for 1 video or about 5/6 sentences.

It’s just a setting your web-developers can change by increasing the amount of height-pixels of that space.

You can compare this to always viewing websites in landscape on your phone, compared to portrait. Zooming in or out does not change this.

Also, why is there no “academy” tag?
